redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: The test-first shell suite imported the not-yet-existing Demo state provider and specified persisted Mock mode, disabled Sign-in, complete reset, route restoration, and contextual-assistance behavior before implementation.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: After adding the provider and shell controls, the focused suite passed all eight issue-specific tests.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Centralized all mutable mock categories and defaults in one typed provider so later destination slices consume the same reset contract.

commands.outputSummary: RED setup was blocked because the fresh worktree had no installed Vitest binary; dependencies were then installed from the unchanged lockfile.

commands.command: corepack pnpm --filter @juli/demo exec vitest run src/__tests__/demo-shell.test.tsx

redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: The first executable suite run found an ambiguous status-role query because the state probe output correctly carries an implicit status role.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: Naming the shell feedback live region made assertions precise while preserving accessible status semantics.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Kept feedback, preview status, and state output as separate named accessibility surfaces rather than weakening role coverage.

commands.outputSummary: 12 passed, 1 failed across the Demo suite; only the ambiguous test query failed.

commands.command: corepack pnpm --filter @juli/demo exec vitest run src/__tests__/demo-shell.test.tsx

redGreenRefactorEvidence.failingTestEvidence: Browser verification checked the real production build at desktop and 375px mobile widths, including deferred client navigation after Manual Refresh.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.passingTestEvidence: Sign-in displayed coming-soon feedback without leaving the route; Manual Refresh moved Settings to /decisions; desktop and mobile layouts preserved four destinations and usable controls.
redGreenRefactorEvidence.refactorEvidence: Responsive CSS hides only the refresh text label at narrow widths while retaining the accessible name, 44px target, focus ring, and reduced-motion behavior.

commands.outputSummary: Demo lint, type-check, all tests, shared-utils tests, and static production build passed.

implementationSummary: Added a shell-owned, typed Demo state provider with Mock persisted as the only enabled mode, a focusable disabled Sign-in control that gives truthful Vietnamese feedback without routing or network access, and a global Manual Refresh that restores every mutable mock-state category before replacing the route with /decisions. Added route-grounded assistive-only Juli guidance, reusable truthful placeholder states with recovery navigation, responsive accessible styling, and eight issue-specific integration tests.
assumptions: The bare /decisions route is the canonical default Recommendations view, matching the Phase 2.6 PRD and issue wording.
Later Decisions, Analytics, and Settings slices will store their mutable Demo state through useDemoState so the global reset contract remains complete.
Sign-in uses aria-disabled instead of the native disabled attribute so keyboard and pointer users can receive the required coming-soon explanation.
risks: Destination feature slices are not implemented yet; their future client components must consume the shared provider rather than introducing private mutable stores.
The local workflow-cache machinery remains ignored and machine-local by repository policy.
